title: Assistant Manager, Data & Impact Analytics
description:
Why This Role Matters

At New Life Stories (NLS), data is not just about numbers — it’s about children, families, and systems of care.
As Assistant Manager, Data & Impact Analytics, you will play a pivotal role in turning programme data into credible evidence, meaningful insights, and compelling impact stories that guide decisions, strengthen accountability, and support advocacy.

This role sits at the intersection of service delivery, impact measurement, and organisational strategy — ideal for someone who wants their data skills to drive real social change.

What You Will Do

1. Data Systems & Infrastructure

You will ensure that NLS has strong, reliable data foundations.

  • Manage and maintain our Case Management System (CMS / Salesforce CRM), including user access and data integrity

  • Set up clear, consistent data entry protocols and documentation

  • Train staff on digital tools to ensure quality and consistency of data

  • Maintain PDPA-compliant data storage and security structures

2. Impact Measurement & Analysis

You will transform raw data into insights that improve programmes and outcomes.

  • Oversee digital assessment tools measuring child and family outcomes (e.g. literacy, resilience, caregiver burden, quality of life)

  • Track programme performance against outcome targets with internal teams

  • Analyse trends and patterns across programmes, including longitudinal outcomes

  • Lead data cleaning, validation, and quality assurance

  • Identify gaps in data processes and co-develop improvements to strengthen measurement systems

3. Reporting & Stakeholder Accountability

You will translate data into narratives that inform, persuade, and comply.

  • Produce impact analytics for donors, funders, and governance reporting

  • Support grant applications and donor reports with robust evidence

  • Develop dashboards, visualisations, and “impact stats” for leadership and partners

  • Contribute core data and insights to the NLS Annual Report

4. Data Governance & Compliance

You will safeguard ethical and responsible data use.

  • Ensure full compliance with PDPA and ethical data standards

  • Monitor and enforce data governance protocols across the organisation

What We’re Looking For

Qualifications & Experience

  • Degree in Statistics, Social Sciences, Economics, Data Science, or related fields

  • Experience in data management, analysis, and reporting

  • Familiarity with impact measurement frameworks and outcome evaluation

  • Prior experience in the social service or non-profit sector is strongly preferred

Skills & Attributes

  • Strong ability to explain data clearly to non-technical audiences

  • Comfortable working with cross-functional and multidisciplinary teams

  • Detail-oriented, organised, and proactive

  • Strong written communication and data storytelling skills

  • Curious, adaptable, and able to keep pace with evolving digital tools

  • A collaborative team player with a strong sense of purpose

Employment type

  • 2-Year Contract Full-time

Why Join New Life Stories

  • Work on meaningful social issues affecting children and families

  • See your data influence real decisions, funding, and systems change

  • Collaborate with practitioners, leaders, and partners across sectors

Be part of an organisation that values rigour, compassion, and integrity

postedCompany.description:
New Life Stories was established in 2014 to prevent intergenerational incarceration. New Life Stories is an IPC (Institution of Public Character) non-profit organisation that aims to provide children of incarcerated parents with essential reading and pro-social skills to ensure that they are not left behind in their crucial early years of development.


New Life Stories has put in place a holistic strategy targeted at providing support to children of the incarcerated and their families. The programmes aim to provide these children with educational and emotional stability so that they can have a fairer start in school. Their parents are also supported by our customised in-prison programmes to prepare them for their release and to give them the reassurance to successfully reintegrate back into society. New Life Stories also educates and guides the families by providing them with essential tools to be self-sufficient.


Befriending


The programme was put in place to provide reading and learning opportunities to children of incarcerated parents so that they can have a fairer start in life. Learning Friends visit the children at their homes weekly to conduct pro-social and reading sessions through storytelling and befriending. Our Learning Friends are a team of trained volunteers, carefully screened for their empathy and mentorship ability to guide the children during their formative years.


In-care 


Trained counsellors visit Changi Prison every week to conduct the In-Care programme where they provide customised self-awareness and resilient building in-prison workshops for the parents. The programmes are designed to restore and strengthen the relationship between parent and child as well as to provide internal and external support to prepare them for times of crisis.


Family Care


New Life Stories will intervene where necessary to provide tailored individual counselling and/or family therapy to help build connectedness within the family. Through activities and games, the sessions help facilitate family strengthening and allows the counsellors to observe behaviours, identify and affirm strengths, model, and encourage new ways of communication within the family. Our case management team aims to strengthen the families own capacities to deal with problems and to cope more effectively in their environment, through a client-centric, goal-oriented, problem-solving approach. Our case workers work towards engaging and assessing the needs of the families and aim to increase their resiliency and improve their quality of life in the long run.
